record.field: Pterodactyl is a free, open-source game server management panel that runs every game server in its own isolated Docker container.

Pterodactyl is a free, open-source game server management panel that runs every game server in its own isolated Docker container.

Built with PHP (Laravel), React and a Go control-plane daemon called Wings, Pterodactyl lets administrators provision, deploy and manage multiple game servers from a single secure web interface, while giving end users a clean dashboard to control their own instances. Container isolation gives each game tailored dependencies, strict resource limits and a reduced attack surface. It is the long-standing standard for hobbyists, gaming communities and commercial hosting providers who run game servers at scale.

Key features
  • Per-server isolation: every game server runs in its own Docker container with strict CPU, memory and disk limits
  • Extensible game support via "eggs" and "nests" — Minecraft, source-engine games, voice servers and hundreds of community-defined templates
  • Multi-node architecture: the Panel orchestrates many remote nodes through the Wings daemon
  • Granular user management with subusers, role permissions, two-factor authentication and per-server SFTP access
  • Live console, real-time resource graphs, scheduled tasks, backups and file manager from the browser
  • Full REST API for automation, billing integrations and white-label hosting platforms
Best for

Gaming communities, server administrators and hosting companies that need to deploy and manage many game servers securely, with multi-tenant access and resource isolation, on their own infrastructure.

services: database: image: mariadb:10.5 restart: always command: --default-authentication-plugin=mysql_native_password volumes: - ./pterodb:/var/lib/mysql environment: MYSQL_DATABASE: panel MYSQL_USER: pterodactyl MYSQL_PASSWORD: null MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: null container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_database' networks: - internal cache: image: redis:alpine restart: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_cache' networks: - internal panel: image: ghcr.io/pterodactyl/panel:latest restart: always links: - database - cache volumes: - ./pterovar:/app/var/ - ./pteronginx:/etc/nginx/http.d/ - ./pterocerts:/etc/letsencrypt/ - ./pterologs:/app/storage/logs environment: APP_ENV: production APP_ENVIRONMENT_ONLY: 'false' CACHE_DRIVER: null SESSION_DRIVER: null QUEUE_DRIVER: null REDIS_HOST: null DB_HOST: null DB_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_PASSWORD} DB_PORT: null MYSQL_PASSWORD: null MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: null DB_CONNECTION: mysql container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T database sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d database # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T database sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
